entry.title: Do you want to learn how to create analytical data models in the SAP BTP, ABAP Environment?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Developing Analytical Models using CDS-based Analytical Projection Views learning journey.

Overview
With this Learning Journey, you will learn how to develop powerful analytical queries using ABAP Core Data Services, that can be consumed by analytical applications. 

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, developers, data engineers, and consultants will be able to create analytical data models in the SAP BTP, ABAP Environment, using CDS-based Analytical Projection Views.

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of ABAP CDS, Basic analytical modeling and querying concepts.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: FSCM Exposure Management 2.0
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

my issue arises in FSCM Hedge management: For Balance Sheet FX risk the exposures are displayed in exposure amount currency and in reporting currency. Is the same possible for exposure management 2.0 with FTREX13 - Exposure Position Flows? There I do see exposures (raw or net) in exposure amount currency only. My customer wants to see the amount in target currency as well. Otherwise with so many different currencies in the system it is difficult for him to determine in advance (before hedging) where the real risk lies…

Regards, Sebastian

entry.title: missing industry code in external supplier registration
entry.content: When creating an external supplier request, we have a hidden field for industry code that is defaulted to example PO category, and this should have been reflected to the external supplier registration but instead the industry code is blank in the said form. Note that there is already mapping for the said field, and this should have been transfer seamless from an external supplier request to external supplier registration form. what seems to be the issue on this?

entry.title: Re: SAF-T for Romania: All you need to know to get started with the solution available in SAP S/4HAN
entry.content:
Dear Ailane,

we started with our Company Code for Austria in 2024. This company has a registration in Romania and has business there , in sap we created just a plant in Romania in our company code of Austria. Therefor we have the abroad functionality in place. So we have to report SAF-T from 2025 onwards to ANAF. 

I went through the customizing instructions and have some quick questions to you. I really appreciate your help.

1. Map payment methods, transaction /CEECV/RO_SAFT_PYMCH

In this customizing table you have to enter here the company code and payment method. Unfortunately it does not allow me to enter our austrian company company code to get the payment methods we also use for the payment of the vendors invoices from Romania. It only allows the payment methods from country Romania and not the ones we created in our Austrian company code. Is there a correction or note to retrieve this?

2. tax types

Is this correct that we assign tax typ 300 'VAT ( TVA)' to our sap VAT keys input and output or do we have to distingues the tax type between input type and output type?

Is the 300 'VAT (TVA' the official tax type of ANAF?

3. Tax keys

is there a list of the tax ANAF SAF-T tax codes to map our sap VAT keys input and out put?

Are those 6 digit tax key eg 310309 '19% output supplies of good and services' the official tax codes from ANAF?. As you can see I was not able to assign all our sap keys to the ANAF keys.

4. Define analyse type

I dont understand this . I have assigned just the profit center. Shall i assign all the others like order, Segment, WBS element, CC too. What is the meaning or impact? 

5. Master data

Unfortunately the customer and vendor master data dont appear in my SAF-T file. I have created the tax category RO8 for vendor and RO9 for customer to assign them in the master data tab identfication. What do I have to enter in the master data tab identification? For example in the vendor master data the tax category RO8 and under tax number long the business partner number? or do I have to enter the VAT registrastion number again which I already have under tax category RO0? Pls see the screenshot.

6. Generell question

Is this correct as a non resident company, located in Austria with business or plant in Romania we are only obliged to report sales and purchaes including master data, but we dont need to report stock and assets?

thanks a lot for your help!

Gunnar

entry.title: Re: Java Cap remote debugging not working with Java 17
entry.content: Hello, i was able to start remote debug, however when disconnecting from vsCode the remote debug connection remain active. VM.stop_java_debugging does not seem to be a valid command. How can the debug be stopped without restarting the application? Kind regards, Stephan

entry.title: Re: ESS- Not able to update Emergency Contact
entry.content: Was this issue resolved?

entry.title: Re: Would i able to do stay certified assessment by purch...
entry.content:
Hi @siva_raman2 
Please access our Stay certified website  where you find the details.
You need an active SAP Learning Hub subscription to take the short assessment to stay certified. You can access preparation material and take the assessment at any time.
SAP Certifications are valid for one year. If your certification was issued before April 2024, it will expire on March 31, 2025.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Query related learning hub course:'Acquiring Core ABA...
entry.content:
Hi @anisen 
Are you using this system our official practice systems: Hands-on Practice for Basic ABAP Programming
There you find the ABAP repository objects mentioned in the exercise description.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills - learning examples (packa...
entry.content:
Hi @Timothy_Dev 
Are you referring to CCER006 SAP Certification Exam, Six Attempts
This is a subscription to SAP Certification exam, six attempts provides you a 12-months access to Certification Hub, where you can schedule up to a maximum of six exam bookings over your subscription period. 
To access the guided, premium learning resources, you need to have either an SAP Learning Hub or SAP Learning Hub, partner edition (available only for SAP partners) subscription.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition kicks off 2025 with a new era of business-empowering, productivity-enhancing and process optimizing innovation – to hear about some of the highlights across the solution, check this video out. As a key part of the Public Cloud ERP, Solution Order Management again delivers new capabilities that help drive increased revenues and growth with optimized solution-based business processes. In this blog, we’ll take a look at the new features being delivered with the 2502 release.

For a brief summary of my top two highlights, take a look at the below video:


Flexible Pricing for Solution Bundles (6BU)

Obtaining higher revenues and growth with solution-based business models results from the ability to combine a range of different types of products in a solution which can be customized and adapted to suit customer needs. A larger scope of more varied offerings could require a high degree of manual input and effort in creating solution orders, so in 2024, we rolled out Advanced Solution Bundling functionality. In a nutshell, solution bundles are pre-configured products consisting of multiple components  packaged together and sold as part of a solution order. To make selling complex & customizable solutions even more efficient, solution bundles are pre-defined and configured in the product master data and can subsequently be added to a solution order, saving solution order specialists time and effort in bundling items. Find out more in this blog post. Revisiting solution bundles in 2502, we have updated them to enable the inclusion of one-time service products which previously weren’t supported with the initial scope.

Notably, with 2502 we have enhanced pricing capabilities to provide greater flexibility to sell items with bundle-specific pricing. Firstly, we have introduced two new price conditions for assigning discounts to solution orders at header level. These define an overall percentage discount based on either the gross amount (price condition type DRG1) or Net amount (price condition type DRN1) of the solution order. The system automatically applies this discount item level, and the discount is applied to all items included in a solution order bundle.

Additionally, we have introduced dedicated pricing for sub-item components within a bundle, increasing flexibility when selling products as part of a bundle or as part of a sales bill of material (BOM) within a bundle. Reflecting whether an item is included in a bundle, this new capability enables you to sell an item at a specific price instead of the standard product price. To support this, new price condition types are now available for solution orders, which can be configured and adjusted in the Manage Prices app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • PSBP (Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are charged as a one-time fee, such as sales and service items.
  • PSMB (Monthly Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are billed periodically, such as service contract items.
  • PSSK (Sales BOM Price) – For sub-items that are part of a sales BOM within a solution bundle.

When an item is added to a solution order as part of a bundle or sales kit, the system automatically determines its price, and the corresponding price condition type is displayed under the price details tab in the item view. Once the solution order is released, the pricing details are carried forward to all follow-up transactions, ensuring a seamless process and reducing manual effort and input errors when handling complex solution bundles with varied pricing structures.

This feature provides greater flexibility in defining pricing strategies for solution selling and enables businesses to offer a broader and more differentiated portfolio of solution orders, ultimately helping to improve business margins. To ensure the new price condition types are available, make sure to update your reference content to the latest version.

Take an in-depth look at the new pricing parameters for solution bundles in the below demo.



Subscription Billing Enhancements (4GT)

Next, we’ll explore the integration between Solution Order Management and SAP Subscription Billing, where we’ve introduced several key enhancements with the 2502 release. These updates are designed to improve the efficiency of creating, modifying, and managing subscription products within solution orders.

With this release, additional fields have been introduced in Solution Order Management to provide greater flexibility and control over subscription-based offerings. When opening the details view of a subscription billing item in a solution order, you will now see a new checkbox named "Create Subscription as Pending." This feature allows the solution order specialist to control the exact timing of subscription activation. If the checkbox is left unchecked, the original activation setting from SAP Subscription Billing will remain in effect, ensuring seamless integration with the existing configuration. However, if the checkbox is selected, the specialist can override the default setting, providing more control over subscription lifecycle management.

Additionally, a new header section for "Subscription Terms" has been introduced. This section enables users to adapt and modify the default subscription terms defined in SAP Subscription Billing directly within Solution Order Management. By doing so, businesses can manage subscription conditions more effectively and govern the entire lifecycle of follow-up subscriptions without requiring separate modifications in the billing system.

 New fields have also been introduced within the billing section, to further enhance flexibility in subscription sales. These include:

  • Billing Cycle and Billing Cycle Reference Date, which help determine how and when the system schedules billing, allowing for better alignment with business requirements.
  • Additional fields that provide the option to define whether recurring charges should be billed together or waived, giving businesses more options when structuring subscription pricing models.

 Several further fields that were previously only available in SAP Subscription Billing are now reflected in Solution Order Management. These include:

  • Withdraw Period End Date
  • Minimum Term End Date
  • Expected Term End Date
  • Expected Term Extension
  • Subscription Pricing Date
  • Waiver Period Number
  • Waiver Period Number Unit

These fields will be available across multiple touchpoints, including the UI, API, and, where applicable, in output management and the migration object of the Solution Order.

Additionally, the UI of the Subscription Item has been redesigned to provide a more intuitive user experience. Enhancements include:

  • Two new assignment groups for terms and billing information, allowing for a more structured and logical organization of subscription data.
  • A more meaningful assignment of both existing and new subscription fields to their respective assignment groups.
  • Improved presentation of negative charges, making it easier to identify and interpret these amounts.
  • Better visualization of recurring prices, ensuring greater clarity in pricing details.

Finally, to round out these improvements, we have added a new column in the Price Calculation View table called "Recurrence." This column indicates the frequency at which recurring charges are billed, making it easier for users to track and manage subscription-based pricing directly from the solution order interface.

With these enhancements, Solution Order Management now offers greater flexibility and control over subscription-based sales, improving efficiency and reducing manual effort when managing complex billing structures. The improved UI and expanded field availability ensure a more streamlined and user-friendly approach to subscription pricing and lifecycle management.

Click the video below to watch an overview of the new Subscription Billing features delivered with 2502:

 New API v4 for Solution Orders(4GT)


SAP S/4HANA Cloud has taken a major step forward in solution order management with the introduction of a new OData V4-based API. This enhancement brings improved performance, efficiency, and integration capabilities, making it easier than ever to manage solution orders effectively.

One of the biggest advantages of this new API is its optimized communication efficiency. By leveraging OData V4, it reduces data transfer payloads and enhances request handling, resulting in faster processing times and lower resource consumption. This translates to quicker response times and a more streamlined user experience.

Additionally, the API supports full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionality, allowing users to efficiently manage solution order items. While individual items within an order can be modified or removed, solution orders themselves remain protected from deletion, ensuring data integrity and maintaining order history.



Contract Accounts in Solution Orders (4GT)

A key new feature introduced in the 2502 release is the support for FI-CA contract accounts in Solution Orders. FI-CA (Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a specialized subledger designed for high-volume billing, invoicing, and receivables management.

With the integration of contract accounts into solution orders, the system retrieves contract account details from the defined payer role and posts the billing information to the FI-CA ledger for further processing. When a payer has a contract account assigned, the system automatically selects it; however, if multiple contract accounts exist for the payer, a popup screen allows users to choose the relevant account from a list.

Contract accounts are maintained both at the header and item levels of the solution order, supporting sales and subscription items. In CFD1 release 1 of 2502, this scope will expand to include service contract items. Notably, different contract accounts can be defined at the header and item levels, allowing for different contract accounts for each solution in cases where multiple solutions are sold in a single order.

When the solution order is released, the specified contract account information is transferred to follow-up documents, which are then processed for billing. Once invoicing is completed, the document is posted to the FI-CA subledger for accounts receivable and payable. From there, users can leverage central contract accounting functions for payment handling, collection management, or clearing open items. The integration of contract accounts into solution orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enhances financial management by enabling precise billing, streamlined payments, and improved oversight through the FI-CA component.

This enhancement centralizes all financial transactions related to solution orders under a single contract account, simplifying tracking and reporting. It also automates billing, improving accuracy, reducing manual effort, and helping prevent invoicing discrepancies. Additionally, supporting contract accounts provides a comprehensive view of customer transactions, speeding up the resolution of any billing or payment queries.

Feedback of Reference Equipment for Service Order Items (4GT)

A central use case of solution order is selling related products under a single order efficiently and with minimal manual input. Supporting this, in 2024 we introduced equipment linkage for service contract items to be associated with a physical good in a solution order. With 2502, we expand this functionality to support non-recurring service items to be linked.

At its core, equipment linkage ensures that when the post goods issue is processed for a sales item, the equipment ID and its corresponding serial number are automatically transferred to the associated service. This is particularly beneficial for serialized physical goods, where every unit has a dedicated equipment ID linked to it during the post goods issue. This association is achieved through Reference Objects, which act as a bridge connecting sales items to their respective services. This automation not only reduces errors but also enhances operational efficiency, ensuring that service activities are well-aligned with the relevant product equipment data.

The link between items is created when you establish an item relationship of "One-Time Service for" or "One-Time Service by" between the sales item and the service item in the solution order. This relationship facilitates the effortless transfer of the equipment number, along with its serial number, from the sales item to the service item. It ensures that all related services are comprehensively equipped to proceed.

The below screenshot shows the item relationship being maintained for a one time service in Solution Order Management. The entire process mirrors the process for equipment linkage for service contracts, which you can see here.

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now!  

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future. 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners): 

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration.  
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys. 

 

 

More Information 

To catch up with the full scope of Solution Order Management features from previous releases, plus further deep-dive content on Solution Business related topics, check out my collection blog.

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.   

   

 

Find All of Our Assets    

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: How to configure decimal places in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Check notes such as 3546932 - Decimal Places are Not Permitted in alternative unit of measure - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Would i able to do stay certified assessment by purch...
entry.content: Thanks Margit .. I understand that but SAP Learning HUB subscription is bit expensive .. do i have to pay $1332.00 to get subscription and toto stay certified? and if i go though CER001 or CER006 then i have to take full exam ? is that correct . Thanks

entry.title: SAM auto-sync parameters
entry.content: This is from the SAM2410 Configuration guide.  Is the Threshold Period definition correct?  I don't get how it is related to the ON_SAVE parameter.  It seems like it should be the threshold for the PERIODIC auto-sync process.  On_SAVE should just happen when the save action is taken.

entry.title: Passing Flag Variables to Adobe Form for Conditional Field Visibility
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I was assigned a task to make two fields in an Adobe Form appear or disappear based on a condition specified in the form’s code. I am passing two flag-type variables to the form, and when one of them is true, one field should appear while the other remains hidden, and vice versa.

I’m wondering if there’s a way to pass these flag values directly from the code to the form? I’ve tried several approaches but haven’t been able to get it to work. We also attempted to validate the condition directly in the form’s JavaScript, but without success.

Currently, the solution we’ve implemented involves adding the flag fields to the form and hiding them. This way, they don’t appear in the form, but we can still access the values stored in the variables.

Thanks in advance 🙂.

entry.title: Re: Passing Flag Variables to Adobe Form for Conditional Field Visibility
entry.content: Another option is handle the conditions in the form context which would effectively allow the target fields to be null when appropriate

entry.title: Future Proof Payroll and Time Event - ’s-Hertogenbosch, the Netherlands
entry.content:
Did you know that labor expenses can account for as much as one-third of your company's total costs? SAP SuccessFactors and SD Worx invite you to the Future Proof Payroll Event, taking place on March 20 at the SAP Office in 's-Hertogenbosch, Netherlands.

Join us to explore how other organizations have streamlined their payroll processes and learn about the crucial role technology plays in this optimization!

Take a look at the agenda here.

Register now!

entry.title: Problems trying to use set_suppress_empty_data
entry.content:
Hi all:

(Running ABAP 7.50)

I’m trying to use the set_suppress_empty_data( abap_true ), but keep getting a dump and an error message (Class DISPLAY_SETTINGS, method SET_SUPPRESS_EMPTY_DATA not supported for CL_SALV_TABLE Control Technology).

My test code is as follows:

REPORT ztest.

DATA alv             TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
DATA display_setting TYPE REF TO cl_salv_display_settings.
" ====================================
" Get some sample data
" ====================================
select * FROM trdir
  where name = 'DEMO_EXPRESSIONS'
  into table @DATA(itab).

TRY.
  " ====================================
  " Just a plain CL_SALV_TABLE
  " ====================================
    cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = alv
                            CHANGING  t_table      = itab ).
    display_setting = alv->get_display_settings( ).
  " ====================================
  " This generates a DUMP (ignores TRY)
  " ====================================
    display_setting->set_suppress_empty_data( abap_true ).

    alv->display( ).
  CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO data(error_cx).
    MESSAGE  error_cx TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
ENDTRY.

The error ignores the TRY…CATCH block.

Any ideas?

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create a QR code directly from Crys...
entry.content: See a review of several options here.

entry.title: Teams integrations affected by MC985483 (New default passcode in CreateMeeting API)?
entry.content:
hi!

does anyone know the impact of this announcement of Microsoft  for teams integration?

https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/announcing-the-deprecation-of-the-ispasscoderequired-property-on-the-microsoft-graph-online-meeting-api/

and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/2155344/will-the-change-introduced-by-microsoft-in-mc98548

this becomes active in 10 days - February 24 2025? 

 

Could not find any documentation on SAP side?

thank you!

A.

entry.title: Re: SAP Single Sign-On: Protect Your SAP Landscape with X.509 Certificates
entry.content:
I am able to get SSO with X.509 working with already distributed personal x.509 client certificates on our clients. A mandatory step in the setup is that either:
- SAP Cryptolib certificate must be signed by our internal PKI. The Root certificate is already distributed to all clients, so the SLC can validate it.
- Add the self signed SAP Cryptolib certificate of the abap system to the windows store on all clients.
Without this step, we get an "A2200210:Peer certificate verification failed" error as described in note 2680913.
So far, so good.

My curiosity is this:
We have enabled SSO for SAPSUPPORT as described in note "3235573 - Service connection "SAP NI with SNC/SSO". SAP Support personel is able to logon to our Abap system without password and with x.509 certificates generated by SAP's internal PKI.
I would expect that also SAPSupport should get a Peer certificate failed error because you don't have access to our root certificate nor access to the self signed SAP Cryptolib certificate.
But apparently you don't get this error. How is that possible? Does your internal support network bypass some security requirements that apply to normal SSO X.509 scenarios?

entry.title: Re: Embedding Machine Learning into SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
We are running S4HANA on Premise (private cloud really) SP01 (02/2023).  What of this stuff still applies. We also have a BTP environment but have not activated any of the SAP AI functionality.

Thanks

Kevin Geiger

entry.title: Public Cloud means continuous innovation and continuous learning – new user group to facilitate both
entry.content:
One of the main benefits of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is that you receive continuous innovations without having to perform upgrades on your systems. Different companies have different ways of managing change, and there are lessons to be learned on the best ways to encourage your business users consume new functionality.

To facilitate sharing of best practices regarding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition usage, we are hosting an online user group that meets every other month where people can share their best practices and we can demo new innovations, either pre- or post-release. Participants can learn from each other and SAP experts, preview new functionality, provide feedback on the latest features, and participate in user research to shape future features.

The Public Cloud Practitioner Forum meets every other month on the third Thursday at 11am Eastern time / 8am Pacific time / 17:00 CET. We welcome customers from any region, as long as you:

  • are live with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • have 25 or more active users
  • want to empower your users with the best possible experience
  • are open to having your end-users participate in occasional user research projects
  • will attend a 1-hour online meeting every other month

If you would like to join, please ask your account team or sign up here:

https://tinyurl.com/public-cloud-prac-forum

(Invitations will be sent within a week of your signup.)

For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Practitoner Forum team, Peter Spielvogel and Esther Vrieze.

entry.title: Re: Problems trying to use set_suppress_empty_data
entry.content:
You only catch CX_SALV_MSG and its subclasses, but not CX_SALV_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED which is not a subclass of this one but of CX_SALV_NO_CHECK.

  • Syntax of CATCH - A CATCH block handles the exceptions of the exception classes cx_class1 cx_class2 ... that are specified after the statement CATCH, as well as the exceptions of the subclasses of these exception classes
  • The method seems only allowed on list and hierarchy sequence, not tree and grids

entry.title: Re: Restrict Visibility of field – Date of Death – in Employee Central to Certain People
entry.content:
What if employees want to view their date of birth but it should be hidden if they view others profile. 

How can I achieve that.

entry.title: Re: ways to start as a Consultant
entry.content:
Hi Omar

These are some points I would like to mention on your questions:

Certification Path:

Indeed, you should take start from Associate level certifications and progress to higher level as gaining experience.

  • Take the C_TS4FI certification exam
  • Certification can lead to career opportunities in the SAP field but if you do not have real experience, it's just a certificate.

SAP Financial Consultant Role:

In my opinion you should aim for an SAP Financial consultant once you have necessary skills and experience, first you need to find out the sources where you can get the training, depending on your budget, search online, when you find one, stick with it, as I told that go to SAP Community forums and get the recommendations from SAP FICO consultants, look for a support role even it's a Junior role, you will learn a lot.

Good Luck my friend😉

Regards,

Syed

entry.title: Re: Hidden Treasure: RAP Feature Tables in the ABAP Keyword Documentation
entry.content:
Hi Andrea

General question:

I wanted to make use of the "Mail Feedback" function, but abap.docu@exchange.sap.corp cloud not be resolved. Is this function only for sap itself? Or how do people make suggestions or feedback to a specific page/finding?

Greetings

Jan Pascal

entry.title: Re: JBOSS 8 Compability
entry.content:
I suggest moving to JBoss 10.

See supported application in CR for Eclipse here:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/09/ea59ef67-887c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

10 seems to be the only one supported along with Java 8.

entry.title: Not option of Country- India in "Mass Upload Utility" app for HSN Creation at Private Cloud,
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We are trying to create HSN code at  at Private Cloud by using  "Mass Upload Utility" app, there is not option of Country "India", How to add Country Country "India" while uploading HSN master ?

Thanks,

Ganesh Bhosale
@Ganesh_Bhosale555 ; @suhas_barge ; @kriti_daga

entry.title: Re: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Direct...
entry.content: Hi tskwin, I came here from Google for the same question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Direct...
entry.content:
Hi tskwin, I came here from Google for the same question.

That said, I don't think there is much difference.

The IAS-route requires more configuration and I think it's the old way, the Local Identity Directory is gaining more traction in the documentation since a few months ago. In fact it doesn't even exist in the NEO documentation.

I would argue that LID is the newer way, which supports newer techniques (like application specific groups), paging and central user logs and other improvements that come with the new SCIM v2 API.

From my talks with the CIS-team and the roadmap, I think it's a new direction they're headed in - allowing for better and easier provisioning.

entry.title: Re: Professional Super User Not Able To Cancel Journal Entry
entry.content: Hi Willian, Is this happening to a particular user or all of them (super users)? Have you tried creating another Super User, assigning it a pro license, and checking whether you can cancel the JE? This is very odd. It might be related to the UI, so if this test doesn't work, you could restore the UI on that specific form. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Restrict Visibility of field – Date of Death – in Employee Central to Certain People
entry.content:
@vianshu I used this rule to achieve the scenario in quotes" employees should be able to view their date of birth but it should be hidden if they view others profile except for people in certain permission group" but part of it doesn't seem to work. People in permission group are able to view if Employees view their own profile DOB is hidden.  

What went wrong here?

entry.title: Re: Data Locking Ownership logs
entry.content:
Thank you Thomas. It appears to be the case of not specific log.

It has come to my knowledge that Recent SAC version upgrade might have caused it? but then we checked across public and private tenant and why only one planning model was affected? so had to rule out upgrade

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi Samuel,

I am not able to join the SAP Signavio community expert session on March 19th. I read those live sessions are not recorded. Is there another session planned later in the year?

Regards

Carl

entry.title: Re: Internationalization Formatter and Type Formatter in...
entry.content:
You need to call the MDK clientAPI formatting functions in your rule.  For currency you would call clientAPI.formatCurrency.

context.formatCurrency(1000,'EUR','it-IT','{maximumFractionDigits:2,useGrouping:true}')
"1.000,00 €"

entry.title: Unable to write workflow container in WF Exit
entry.content:
Hey there!

In a workflow definition I added my own multi-line Container that I want to use in a form task. I set the flags IMPORT and EXPORT alternating with always the same effect.

I have added a workflow exit in a task by adding my class in tab programming exits.

This exit will be called several times: before, while and after processing the work item.

at event BEF_EXEC Before Execution I can set values to the wf container with the following code:

 

 

 

DATA(wf_container)         = im_workitem_context->get_wf_container( ).
CASE im_event_name.
  WHEN swrco_event_before_execution.
    lt_mydata = VALUE #( ( field1 = '01' field2 = '02' ) ).
    TRY.
        wf_container->set(
            name       = 'ZMYDATA'
            value      = lt_mydata  ).
      CATCH ...
    ENDTRY.
 ENDCASE.

 

 

 

But: at event AFT_EXEC After Execution I get the exception CX_SWF_CNT_CONT_ACCESS_DENIED

The exception is raised in CL_SWF_CNT_CONTAINER->IF_SWF_CNT_ELEMENT_ACCESS_1~ELEMENT_SET_VALUE_REF which indicates that the container element is only readable and maybe because "element exists locally"?

But why is it possible to set the element value at BEF_EXEC?

 

**** element exists locally.
    IF <element>-props O swfcn_p_read_only.
      cx_element_ro l_elem_ro me name.
      cx_exception_return l_elem_ro.
    ENDIF.

 

Any ideas what I am doing wrong?

Thanks 
~Enno

entry.title: Re: Advanced intercompany Sales - IC Supplier Invoice not...
entry.content: i have solved my issue, check  that PO number is correctly filled in PO reference number in CBIC sales order

entry.title: How to get HANA Cloud Table Columns in camel case instead of uppercase on CAP Entity Deployment
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am following the CAP Tutorial to create Database Entities --> https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-cloud-cap-create-database-cds.html .

 

However, the HANA Cloud table columns are coming only in UPPERCASE. Is there a way to get them in Camel Case?

entry.title: Re: Which KPI's do you have in software development?
entry.content: @holm What is a sotography?

entry.title: Work around for WTPA forms for languages other than English
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

AS WTPA forms are available in English only, what is the approach customers have to follow if they have languages other than English? Do they need to use WTPA forms in English only or they have to use WTPA forms outside the system for all languages including English?

entry.title: Automatic OP clearing when using an alternative payer
entry.content:
Hello community,

I have the following challenge.
We only use FI in the public cloud. We receive the invoices via interface from a pre-system. The open items and payments are then managed in the public cloud.
We are increasingly working with payment advice notes and automatic clearing of open items. In this scenario, we have the problem that we have different payers. In other words, the customer is the invoice recipient, but the payment advice note and payment come via a different payer. (e.g. central settlement) The open item is for the customer, we have filled the “Alternative payer” field in the business partner with the corresponding payer. We had hoped that when processing the payment advice note, the system would automatically recognize that the alternative payer wants to settle the customer's corresponding open item.
Unfortunately, this does not work.

We have only defined the business partners with the role FLCU00 (customer) and do not use any sales data, and therefore no partner roles in the system.

Does anyone have experience with the “alternative payer” function or with the scenario I have described?
I would be grateful for any ideas.

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

Alex

entry.title: Re: How to get HANA Cloud Table Columns in camel case ins...
entry.content:
May be this helps:

quoted-table-name-quoted-column-names

entry.title: Re: How to get HANA Cloud Table Columns in camel case instead of uppercase on CAP Entity Deployment
entry.content: why that bothers you?

entry.title: Re: Attach document to Signavio
entry.content: i want to be able to send someone a signavio diagram and have attached documents (an excel spreadsheet and a url, specifically) for them to access. basically one place for them to view/retrieve all information relating to the process diagram. any ideas on how this is accomplished?

entry.title: Re: Problems trying to use set_suppress_empty_data
entry.content: Raymond: "The method seems only allowed on list and hierarchy sequence, not tree and grids". Thanks!! That's the answer I needed. The TRY...CATCH portion is, in fact, not as important. The code depicted was just a quick and dirty test for the set_suppress_empty_data() method. Thanks again, Luis

entry.title: Re: Show Video in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/docs/BUILD_APPS/d65729a1d2574b078798129950cb4d5c/open-video-player?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Latest

entry.title: CALM for implementation - Import order of many TR that belong to the same feature
entry.content:
Hi,
we are implementing SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems and I've found in the online documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/CloudALM/08879d094f3b4de3ac67832f4a56a6de/5aa24f076e3b4b47839f762baa7d089a.html) these recommendation:

- We recommend avoiding manual import activities in CTS with transports managed by SAP Cloud ALM.

- Do not perform buffer manipulations in CTS with transports managed by SAP Cloud ALM. There is no self-healing mechanism available.

TMS parameters is configured for NO_IMPORT_ALL and IMPORT_SINGLE_ONLY to value 1.

In the same help "Deploying Transports of a Feature" is reported:

- Caution: In the Feature app, the transports are sorted by their ID for better clarity. This is not the order how the transports were imported. The import order is always determined by the order in the respective TMS transport buffer.

 

My doubts are:
1) I have a feature with many TR (for example 20-30 or more) and decide to transport into test or prod. It seems import order is related to transport buffer (so it depend on the release date of TR for QAS): is it true?
Since TMS parameters do not allow "full truck" it seems import order will follow order in transport buffer.
But there is no guarantee that this is the correct order to avoid import error.
What tool/rules determine the correct sequence for avoiding import errors of a feature?

In the documentation they do not recommend to change manually buffer manipulations or import manually TR.

Regards
Maurizio

entry.title: Re: How to Display Quick View in The Table column with SmartLink navigation using CDS Annotations
entry.content:
Hi appu.john,

"Customer" is a standard semantic object. Is there any way to create a custom semantic object and assign the same way?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How can I connect my SAP BTP Kyma with my On-Premise...
entry.content:
check this out:
https://blogs.sap.com/2022/02/24/extending-on-premise-systems-via-kyma-runtime/

entry.title: Re: How to get HANA Cloud Table Columns in camel case ins...
entry.content: I have seen this but this is to map to already existing tables. Will it create new tables when deployed?

entry.title: Re: CALM for implementation - Import order of many TR tha...
entry.content:
Hi, Maurizio,

First, I would mention that Cloud ALM is not 'performing' the import.  It simply instructs TMS to do this.

My understanding is that TMS performs the import based on the order the TRs enter the buffer of the target system (date/time stamp of arrival there). 

It is important that changes are made and released in a timely fashion to avoid problems where later TRs release prior to former ones, where there is some inter-dependence, and could end up importing in a wrong order, based on those release dates.

This is not anything that has changed due to Cloud ALM.  Solution Manager would have had this same exposure for order.

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: CL_SALV_TABLE Hide empty rows
entry.content:
Hi all,

Does anybody knows if there is a method to hiding (i.e. Setting not visible) empty rows when using CL_SALV_TABLE (Similar to CL_GUI_ALV_GRID_EXT=>SET_HIDE_EMPTY_COLUMNS)

Thanks,

entry.title: Based on my available Raw Material, I wish to know how much I can produce of my Finished Goods?
entry.content:
Hello all,

My client requested to know based on how much raw material I have in stock, how much can I produce of Finished Goods?

Let's say we have the following scenario:

The customer requested 100 Kg of Finished Goods A.
We go to check and we don't have enough Raw Material to produce 100 kG of Finished Goods A (I have 10 Kg of Raw Material and to produce 100 Kg of Finished Goods I need 20 kG)
I wish to know, based on my available Raw Material, how much can I produce of my Finished Goods A. (In the case of 20 kG of Raw Material, I can only produce 50 kG of Finished Goods).

Basically I need SAP to calculate and tell me how much Finished Goods I can produce based on my available Raw Material.

I understand this is something to be developed, can you please help me on the best way to approach this request to the ABAP?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content: Kindly consider as I’m being at 31 in attendee list.

entry.title: Re: SAP ALM Tasks Synchronization with CBC Projects
entry.content:
Hi,

I found a case from this week that seems to describe the same problem you are reporting here. 

Development responded:

We have identified an issue of task status synchronization from CBC system. Now the issue has been fixed, please verify. -> hopefully it is also resolved for you.

In case you still encounter the problem, you should create a case in component SV-CLM-IMP-TSK so development can review and advise.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Remove Back button in Work Zone when app opens in a new tab
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a few Work Zone applications which open in a new tab and would like to hide the standard back button in that case; the reason being that when we click the back button in the new tab, we're back to the Home page in addition to the original Home page from where we executed the application in the first place.

Pic attached for reference.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: BI 2025 Countdown with Eric Fenollosa & Gregory Botticchio
entry.content:
There are still a lot of questions about this widx-format.
In my opinion it is not that great, because it is a proprietary file-format and the user probably has to install some kind of software like the Client-Tools before to open and interact with this widx-file.
More importent is the abilty to export the complete report-structure/data as html-content/file(s) so the recipient can use it with nothing but a browser.

entry.title: Re: CALM for implementation - Import order of many TR tha...
entry.content: Hi Paula, so Cloud ALM only instructs TMS to do import of TR based on buffer queue; if there is an issue in the import order how can I modify this? In the ufficial documentation they do not recommend to perform buffer manipulation or manual import. As you know in a S4 Hana project there many teams that work together on different features and create different TR. Normally QAS or Pre-Prod is used to revise the correct import order for PRD system. I know that transport checks can be useful to anticipate conflicts, but if the import in the QAS is only linked to release date (it cannot be changed by Clould ALM and is not recommended to ajust buffer manually), which is the right way to solve and prevent import issues with CALM? Regards M.

entry.title: Re: How to extend a standard CDS view for new fields
entry.content:
@Khabir_Raja 

At line 5, replace VBAP with the CDS Entity name which is I_SALESDOCUMENTITEM.

How can I remove this?

entry.title: Change Message Error to Warning
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I'm facing a range error NR753 when I'm trying to edit field NF Number (NFNUM) in Nota Fiscal form in Tcode J1B2N

I need to edit this field for some internal needs of the company regarding NFSe manual entries.
I'm not able to edit, I tried to include the error on tables user control T100SA and T100S and change it to W but I'm not getting success, the error remains .


Is it possible to change in somewhere else ? Or is it not possible to change this type of error to a warning?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP CDS Access Controls in Projection Views
entry.content:
Hi,

you can define access control for projection view:

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Access mgr 3'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZACI_EMP {
    grant
        select
            on
                ZI_EMP
                    where
                         Mgr = 3;
                        
}

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Mgr 3'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZACC_EMP {
      grant 
        select
            on ZC_EMP where inheriting conditions from entity ZI_EMP;
                        
}

 

 

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/abencds_f1_cond_inherit.htm#!ABAP_VARIANT_2@2@ 

 

You can also check the new feauture for Version 7.58
https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abencds_dcl_role_proj_rule.htm

entry.title: Check the Onboarding Status of your systems in Customer Insights reporting
entry.content:
During the system onboarding process some mandatory steps have to be performed on customer side to set the systems live. This new section displays information about the onboarding project”  from the “System Setup” to “System go-live”, and tracks progress.

The Onboarding section is based on the executed SAP EarlyWatch reports and it is now available for the SAP IBP product group. SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP BTP are planned to be added.

The section visualizes in the graph all six milestones for each pair of systems to be onboarded. It allows to closely monitor the progress of each milestone and easily identify areas that may need your attention. 

You can see more details to each milestone status by clicking on the appropriate segment of the graph. This detailed breakdown ensures that every aspect of the project implementation is transparent and accountable, facilitating more effective project management and decision-making. If the milestone rating is "Red" (Overdue) you can click on the link to navigate to the SAP EarlyWatch report to check the required actions. 

 

Rating of the milestones
There are 6 milestones to be processed for the onboarding:

  1. System Setup: the test system is newly set up, there are no real users yet in the system.
  2. Access Test System: the test system is starting to get used by customer users. There are at least two logons to the Excel Add-in in the test system within one week or at least 100 OData calls to the app „Planners Workspace“.
  3. Start Data Load: the test system usage increases. The app „Data Integration Jobs“ has at least 100 OData calls and the HANA index server memory utilization peaks at least 20% within one week.
  4. Access Prod System: the productive system starts to get used. There are at least two logons to the Excel Add-in within one week or at least 100 OData calls to the app „Planners Workspace“.
  5. Start Cut-Over: cut-over activities are executed from the test system to the productive system. There are at least 100 OData calls to the app „Import Software Collection“ in the productive system.
  6. System Go-Live: activity in the productive system increases significantly. There are at least 300 OData calls to either the app "Manage Search Models" or the "Cloud System Management App" within one week. Additionally, the number of OData calls to these apps must have increased by at least 150 times compared to the previous week, or there are at least 30 Excel Add-ins logins within one week, with logins increasing by at least 15 times compared to the previous week.

The milestone date provides the date of when each milestone was achieved on the respective system ID. SAP EWA calculates an average threshold how long a milestone can be processed overall and compare the threshold with the real duration. Based on this a  milestone will be rated as:

  • Reached (Green): the milestone has been successfully achieved.
  • Not Reached (Grey): the project is ongoing, the milestone is not yet reached, but still within the expected timeline.
  • Overdue (Red): the milestone was not reached within the estimated timeline. When at least one milestone is rated "Red" the whole project will be displayed as "Red" / Overdue.

The table below the graph provides an overview with additional details across all onboarding systems including Milestone Threshold,  Days since Previous Milestone, Project Rating and link to the SAP EarlyWatch report. The table can be exported.

  • Milestone Threshold for the calculation of the  “Overdue” rating is calculated dynamically, ensuring that the least progressive 25% of customers are rated as “Overdue”.
  • Days since Previous Milestone represents the number of days between two consecutive milestone dates. Please note: If milestones are not reached in the sequential order, the "Days since Previous Milestone" will display a negative value.
  • Current Project Rating indicates the overall rating for the onboarding project :
    • In Progress: the onboarding project is ongoing, no milestone is rated as “Overdue”.
    • Completed: all six milestones have been successfully achieved.
    • Overdue: the estimated timeline for reaching one of the milestone was exceeded.  

To focus only on the critical projects you can use the filter "Current Project Rating" and select only projects with the "Overdue" status.

Please note: Due to the temporary restriction to just one product group (SAP IBP), this section is not displayed by default. Use the Personalization option in the top right of ‘Customer Insights’ dashboard to make it visible.

 

SAP for Me is made for you!

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

Related blogs:  all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting.

entry.title: Remote source definition
entry.content:
We are trying to refresh a lab database from another system but would like to keep the definitions of the remote source in tact.  Is there a process to store off the definitions and then import after a system has been refreshed from a backup of another system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Remote source definition
entry.content:
For exporting from Smart Data Access you could save the virtual tables and import them after the refresh. 

List the remote sources

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/924e41fc417741fb9920705f15a8fbe0.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2.0.08

Export/Import the virtual tables:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/244edb2f08da47c4b8924b8bed710910.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2.0.08

entry.title: Setup SQI adapter connection to on-oremise SQL DB servers
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

As part of the SAP PI/PO to SAP BTP integration suite migration, I have a requirement to create MS SQL JDBC connection to on-premises MS SQL DB server in SAP Cloud Connector.

I am facing a challenge while creating a connection while following the naming convention as < SQL server >\<DB Instance>.

Please find below details and if anyone had faced such issues, kindly assist and if there is any workaround for this available also would be helpful.

 Business scenario:

Create a MS SQL JDBC connection to on-premises MS SQL DB server when the MS SQL server string is as: <SQL server>\<DB instance>. Example: SDESEQSQL191\SEQ2TEST

Configuration steps:

Create a TCP connection to a non-SAP system in SAP Cloud Connector
Create JDBC Data Source in Integration Suite.
Symptom:

When setting up the Access Control entry in SAP Cloud Connector, in the step to add the internal host and port, SAP Cloud Connector does not allow the naming convention as <SQL server>\<DB instance> throwing the error message below “Invalid or missing input”

SAP cloud connector version is: 2.17.2

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Rakesh Yadav

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment Cloud Connector SAP Integration Suite #SAP PI/PO Migration

entry.title: Service Call Status History for KPI reporting
entry.content:
Hello experts.

Are there any existing reports in SAP B1 that will display the statistics of service call statuses, in particular how long service calls are in a particular status?

If not, how does one query for this information when a service call may be updated but the status is not changed? I am needing to create a query for a customer to run weekly that returns service call information when the service call has had a certain status applied within a date range?

I cannot wrap my head around how OSCL & ASCL can be pulled together, even with union to give me unique results.

For example…
Service call #1 gets status 7 applied on Monday. The status of service call #1 then gets changed to 10 on Tuesday, then gets changed to 15 on Wednesday.

Service call #2 gets status 7 applied on Monday but the status of service call #2 doesn't get changed within same week.

Service call #3 gets status 7 applied on Tuesday. Service call #3 is updated on Wednesday but the status isn't changed. Service call #3 then gets status 11 applied on Thursday.

I need a query that pulls the time stamp of when status 7 was applied to a service call within a date range, regardless of the current status of the service call.
With the example above, I should see a result set with 3 service calls and 2 have a timestamp for Monday and 1 has a timestamp for Tuesday.

Thank you in advance for any help.

ES

SAP Business One

entry.title: Re: Generate PDF Form binary with script in SDK
entry.content:
Hello,

It seems to work with a custom form, so it is okay now.

 

This function doesn't work with standard forms or is it fossible that I didn't get the right formTemplateCode ? If so, where can I find it ?

 

Thank you,

Marion

entry.title: Cross xsa application (node modules) access
entry.content:
I am trying to build cross application access, or there are two projects deployed on the same XSA space. 

The app A has db, node and ui modules and in the node, it has web service which reads or updates some data from the db module. 

There is another app B which has only node and ui modules. (no db modules and I don't want to create db module just to be able to solve my problem with creating cross container access, because I would have to create whole HDI container just for reading one record from remote table).

Using the Enable Applications to Perform Tasks with the Authority of Other Applications I tried to build the cross app access, but still no success.

Any ideas or suggestions are very welcome.

Best Regards,

Simona

They had an ABAP plugin to analyse also SAP systems, not sure if this is still available in Sonargraph.

entry.title: Re: Behavior validation messages are not displayed
entry.content:
The code shown worked for me on a SAP BTP ABAP Environment system (latest release 2502).

I had however to perform a few changes because when using cut and paste syntax errors were shown.

1. Not sure why we see a "slash" here:

READ ENTITIES OF /zi_product IN LOCAL MODE

2. In the BDEF in the validation statement a semicolon is missing

validation validateMandatoryFields on save { field ProductName create; }

must read

validation validateMandatoryFields on save { field ProductName; create; }

And I saw

 

Question: 
You have not mentioned on which release you are working ?

entry.title: Re: End to End Designing the Multilevel Hierarchy in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Kartheek,

finally I copied the relevant CDS view and applied the changes explained in note 3208160 for the CDS annotations "Consumption.filter.mandatory Boolean (true, false)".

And it worked as desired.

It is quite likely that the issue is caused by the S/4 version we are using at the moment. I did not want to apply patches in S/4 because we start an upgrade soon.

Thank you very much for your blog.

Br.,

DirkO

entry.title: Re: Reverse Tax Engine - Payroll USA
entry.content:
Thanks for the reply @Matt_Fraser!

Just hoping anyone who does have MA would respond so we confirm if we missed something in our config.  We didn't see any issues until MA changed their tax tables for the surtax and it only popped up with our 2025 payrolls.

entry.title: Re: Single Cycle Plan
entry.content:
Hello @Cdixon40 ,

here you go:

1. When creating a single cycle plan is there an option to pick a strategy?: No, it is not possible. You have to create a strategy plan. 

2. Is difference between a single Cycle plan and a Quality plan?: Quality plan is used to trigger tasks which are used to inspect a material while GR, GI and various goods movements. On the other hand Single cycle plan is used to do time based or performance based maintenance of equipment. Quality plan is not created for equipment. 

3. What is single cycle plan mostly used for? Single cycle plan is used for time based or performance based maintenance. For ex: Monthly maintenance of a motor, maintenance after 10000 km od distance travelled by a vehicle or 5000 hr of running hours.

Let me know if you need information.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Controlling Sales to Blocked Customer in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi SAP SD Experts,

In SAP (ECC), we have one customer (SH) with two different SAP numbers and are selling to them from two different entities. The problem is that I have blocked one customer number in entity one, but the other entity is still selling to the same customer, even though they are blocked, due to the different SAP number. How can I control this scenario?

Say - A -Customer 

Company code -TR10-(Customer number is 0001 For customer A)

Company code-PL10 (Customer number is 0002 For customer A)

If blocked customer A(001) in CC TR10, system allowed me to sale to customer 0002 from CC PL10 due to different SAP number.

And not possible to extend the customer from TR10 TO PL10 Receiving error below

I am facing the same error; I have used destination and connectivity service and bound to application.

If you able to solve this. Please help me on this. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content: @MartinRaepple @H_Ettelbrueck Hi, trying to set up a new IAS tenant with OIDC integration with Entra ID following this blog.  In IAS Admin Console, Application > System Applications > Administration Console shows "Protocol Type: SAML2.0".    How to change it to OIDC?

entry.title: Re: Steps to install an ADD-ON using SAINT
entry.content: Transaction SAINT main advantage against importing 3rd party transport is: As of Version 0053 of the SAP Add-On Installation Tool, not only can you install ABAP add-ons, but you can also uninstall them again -> https://me.sap.com/notes/2011192

entry.title: Re: iOS native app - Offline OData DataFormat
entry.content: Are you using MDK or building a Native iOS App using the BTP SDK for iOS?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Mobile Services Native/MDK App Authorization...
entry.content: MDK currently only supports OAuth2 for it's authentication mechanism.  We do not support an anonymous user connection.  Authentication is handled by the IdP configured in BTP so you can utilize an IdP either through IAS or another that would handle the authentication.

entry.title: Re: how to change Length of G/L Account Number in custom...
entry.content:
Extend the view and adjust the GLAccount field length in your extended view.

Either use Cast or ltrim or by using a custom data element.

entry.title: Re: How to pass dynamic value through a mdk Extension code
entry.content:
If you are already in extension code, couldn't the extension just directly invoke the document editor extension?

In terms of "passing data", normally you pass data into the extension via the Extension Properties.  Controls don't normally pass data back to anything.  They may expose a getValue() method so that an action like create entity can grab a value from the extension to save into a backend transaction (offline or online). 

From your description you will have to determine how you want to handle the scenario.  Since document sizes can vary widely you may want to look at writing a temp file to the app sandbox Documents or temp directory.  You could also consider using AppClientData to pass something as well.

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation
entry.content: Thanks Dan.  That worked for me.  Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Re: Cross xsa application (node modules) access
entry.content: a few years ago, i was trying the same thing, no luck, ended up binding all the app to the same uaa as workaround.

entry.title: Re: Automatically nudge sending for onboardee
entry.content: Hello! I have the same problem. Were you able to find a solution? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Call for Speakers (SIT DFW) - spring 2025
entry.content: For my part, I'd be happy to talk on any one of a range of topics:  Internal experimental use of Agents and LLMs inside SAP Community, tips on running LLMs locally, or a review of the 2025 SAP Developer Survey Results.

entry.title: Re: I created the SAP GUI AI Agent
entry.content:
Hi,

Honestly, I would like to everything about it. 😀

  1. deeper understanding of how the agent uses tools
  2. Like to see the program code
  3. If you have tried different versions of SAP GUI

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules (5/5)
entry.content: @ranjeetkumar07 oh. so what should be used to define agents when we use custom workflow ?? thanks

entry.title: Re: The fastest way to a deployable CAP app on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
@daniel_schlachter 

Thank you for your reply. I have updated the cds-dk and now, the global version is 8.7.2.

I've learned that the leading slashes are removed during the build if the following parameter is specified in mta.yaml:

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo

Although the parameter is there, the manifest.json in the dist folder still contains a leading slash.

entry.title: Re: The fastest way to a deployable CAP app on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
@Willem_Pardaens 
Thank you for your comment. I have tried `cds-ts add workzone`, but it didn't add the transpile task either. Additionally, it produced the following error during the build:

Error: app/booksui3/webapp/Component.ts(1,27): error TS2307: Cannot find module 'sap/fe/core/AppComponent' or its corresponding type declarations.

I have yet to identify whether this is caused by `cds add typescript` or `cds-ts add workzone`.
